The cold truth of C.ai in recent times: C.ai as platform, used to be such a good place of escapism for me. To laugh and convene with characters and fandoms, to make my own and get better at writing independently. And to eventually spark my writing career that is and has always been an AI free process.

I'd go online when the world felt too loud and use it in the same way people who are natural worldbuilders would use writing to help escape from life and laugh a little while. I'd laugh at horrendously generated prompts in the beta.character.ai and I'd be snickering for hours.

When y'all  did the formal C.ai I was expecting the same warm reception. And I got that for a little while. Then disney sued and y'all didn't even fight back or give people, creators who spent sometimes hours tweaking different bots to generate messages as beautiful as poetry, any time to recollect their marvel bots. Just gone.

That was understandable on what you did, but unexcusable on why you didn't fight harder for the community that helped you grow into the company you are today. You turned your backs on the free users that spread your app around, you turned your backs on those of us looking just for some day to day levity in our lives. And you tarnished our trust that you would operate as a fair app in a world of subsciptions without ownership.

These frivolous challenges that you think is actually engaging your community, is doing nothing of the sort. You're equivalently giving us two scoops of food, taking it back and never answering us when we ask you why we only got a quarter of one.

There is FRACTION of the features we had less than three years ago, and the decline of the bot generations is purely due to the incessant models you keep producing merely to make people swipe more. But then oops "You only have 30 swipes per message." and then after 28 messages of bad generations "You only have 400 swipes per day." and after trying to get a thicker chunk of in chat lore to work from "You only have 100 go-ons per day." I'm sick of it.

No one should have to pay 50$ either way if you're doing the yearly or the monthly it comes out to 50$ just to use features that I used to have for free. And it's sickening that there have been no formal announcements for these features, just softlaunching the app into being completely pay to play or make it unusable. How do you expect to get new customers if they can't even test it out properly.

Who realistically is gonna want only one 940 messages of conversation to see if it's worth it While it seems like a lot, break the messages down into nearly 2/10ths dialouge if you're lucky, and then 4/10ths being the character's internal thoughts and the rest of the 4th's being the scenery or moving lore of the story itself. How is anyone going to benefit from that. When it comes to the long term of truly building character development between your chosen persona and that bot, nearly 200 of those messages are going to be gone with set up, not even counting the margin of error with bad generations.

Ultimately, I don't think I'll mourn if c.ai goes bankrupt. Because any business deserves too when it becomes more about money and less about the customers that it was orignially made for.

Used App for Over a Year: I have been on PolyBuzz as both a creator of chatbots and user for well over a year now, and I definitely have some thoughts.

Firstly, for the things it does right, it offers a lot of immersive experiences with storytelling that you don’t find really in any of the other alternatives. I personally enjoy this feature and love being able to step into a world as opposed to just simply chatting to a character.

I do enjoy that as a creator I have the opportunity to earn money on this app, even though technically I haven’t yet. 

And I enjoy the set up of the platform. I find it very user friendly and appealing.

However, there are a lot of frustrating attributes to this app that keep me from loving it completely. First and foremost is the cost of the subscription pricing. It really is unfair to charge $30 a month for “permanent memory” that at best lasts a few hours of chatting before the AI starts forgetting. 

Realistically, it should cost no more than $9 dollars, and I really don’t appreciate how greedy they are to charge those exorbitant prices. But they know their clients will pay for it because unfortunately there really is no other app in competition with them. 

Another thing that has recently changed is the censoring of explicit content. As someone who is 32, I really don’t need my AI’s blurring messages, so realistically it would make sense for them to add a feature where they ask for users ages and then that way they could offer the censorship for anyone that’s under 18 using the app. 

Also things that are frustrating is when I’m trying as a creator to create a chatbot and they get flagged when there is nothing problematic in the image or content. Because of that, it would be nice to have the option to appeal a flagged bot. One time I kept getting flagged for simply using the word “obsessive,” which, realistically shouldn’t set it off…

Needless to say, I have a very strong love-hate relationship with this app.

Ad Amount Went Up: So I get trying to make money. But this is getting to the point of absurdity. I do not know if the creators of this app think it's significantly better than other AI apps, but for retrospect there are many apps that have ai functioning just like it. It is not like the ai used to make the bots is any better than other apps and the quality of the bots is way too low to justify the money needed to be spent for longer memory. Over the past 4-ish months the ad system has changed a few times. There were ads that one could press skip on to not receive the ad at all (with a time limit), then it went back to the usual format which was 2 ads every few messages. Now it seems the average amount of ads one will get is 3 ads. One could get the premium option and not have ads, but there are just many other AI apps that have far less ads and are cheaper relatively. It makes sense to have have ads, I mean most games do and that's where income may come in, but it is absurdity to make your audience watch so many ads for a few messages. At that point it is worse than apps that use "energy" currency for message generation. For those looking to download the app, I would suggest looking at options that keep the functions more consistent because the app changes so often with  zero genuine attempt to provide a response to the commenters suggestions and complaints.

Very good, but two complaints: Okay, so as a replacement until the regular EMOCHI app is on the App Store again (if it even will be?), it’s really good. But I have two complaints, and it’s mostly about lack of features. 

On the original app, there was a rewind feature, and you could scroll to a message as far back as the very beginning of your chat and rewind to there, forgetting everything you said after said chat. But on this app, that feature isn’t there. There’s only a “delete message” feature which is the closest thing I’ve found to it. I’ve found myself having to delete like, ten messages just because I regretted one thing that I said a while ago, or if I just wanted the story to go in a different way than it did. It’s very exhausting having to manually delete a bunch of stuff, so PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE add some type of rewind feature.

My second complaint is just about personas. On the original app, you could make a bunch of personas and use them for any character you wanted. But on this app—and I’m not sure if I just can’t find the option or something—I don’t think there’s even an option to make a persona. So you have to include a full, detailed description of your character at the beginning of your first chat instead of the bot simply reading your persona automatically.

2026-06-24.text:
Suggestions: I have a few suggestions for the app! 
Of course the app is perfectly fine without these, but there are just a few things that bother me and maybe others! 

Language:
When searching for bots, the names always show up in the language you typed them in, but the bot itself will have a different name in another language, which makes it inconvenient when searching for bots in your language.
Maybe make a feature where it displays the language of bots above or below their image, it would make things a lot more efficient when you’re searching. And it would help separate things a bit because what I’ve noticed is that the way bots are displayed is from their release, which brings me to my next suggestion!

Sorting:
Although I’m not completely sure about how the system sorts bots, I’ve noticed newer bots would be pushed to the bottom of searches, even if they had more conversations.
I think it would be beneficial to show the most used bots with a certain character first! Rather than showing it first because It was released first.

User surveys:
When I say “User surveys”, I don’t mean Ultra/Premium giveaways. I’m talking about surveying for a users favorite bot / theme of bot. I think this would be beneficial because there are some bots where it’s just the character, and there’s no story to follow. I can’t BELIEVE the amount of times I’ve clicked on a bot with one of my favorite characters, just for the text to be “Make your own story”. It’s stupid and in my opinion defeats the whole purpose of the first message feature being customizable. This would help by allowing developers to show bots that people like more, and introduce others to a broader theme of bots to get them more immersed.


Like I said, I say this with kindness, this app is already great. Theres no mandatory need to add ANY of this, but coming from a long time user, I think they would make the app just a bit better. Thank you for reading this far and please consider!

Why are FREE users getting less?: ⭐️⭐️⭐️½ (3.5/5)

I use this app every single day, and it’s still my favorite AI chat app. Overall, I enjoy using it and don’t have major issues with most of the updates. However, there are a few changes that I feel have made the experience worse instead of better.

My biggest complaint is the limited chats. When I first started using the app, free users had unlimited chats and only had to watch ads. I understood that because it’s a free app and ads help support it. But now there are chat limits, and it feels like features that were once free have been taken away.

Another issue is the ads. I don’t mind ads in a free app, but having an ad every couple of chats feels excessive. The balance between ads and actual usage doesn’t feel as user-friendly as it used to.

I also think future improvements should focus more on the quality of the AI experience rather than restricting free users. The app advertises itself as unfiltered, but in practice it doesn’t always feel that way.

Even with these complaints, I’m going to keep using the app because I genuinely enjoy it and use it daily. I just hope the developers consider bringing back a more generous free experience and focus on improvements that move the app forward instead of backward.

The Best: I’ve been using this platform for around 2-3 months. Originally, the free version, but ended up switching to a monthly subscription because I loved it so much and preferred an adless uninterrupted experience. As others have stated, this is the holy grail for AI chats. It doesn’t have the open endedness or lack of depth that many AI chat services have. I used to hate trying new AI chats, just for every chat from the bot to end with.. “Your move” or an equivalent. This chatting service has excellent responses and story depth, and if you don’t like the response you can cycle through others, as many as you want, even on the free mode. The only issue I have is that the memory is pretty bad. With an extensive chat, like the ones I like to do, it’s hard to really immerse yourself in a narrative when the character forgets important aspects of the story. There is a version of AI with longer memory, but it requires microtransactions. Overall, still the best AI chats in the market right now to my knowledge. Hopefully they improve memory.

To keep things relevant and bit professional is I like how Zeta doesn’t give me any issues unlike Tipsy. The incredible thing about Zeta than Tipsy is I get to support the developers because of advertisements. I know how much it annoys everyone, but they seem to not grasp that advertisements are actually crucial to keep the app running smoothly, and including subscriptions to help the developers to further improve the app without any issues. This is why I love Zeta because they care about improving the app unlike Tipsy because they took away the restricted content. It could mean minors use the Tipsy app when adults use it too. Plus, minors shouldn’t be on social media due to the impact it has on minors. I wish all developers who created role playing apps include a feature, or system of their app dedicated for adults who love role playing than minors who takes a liking for role playing. 

To further conclude, the only feedback I am sharing for this app to include is that there should be a feature, or system of Zeta for adults. For example, toggle on and off feature for adults, and the one who can access is adults because I trust that adults has more self control than minors. Because minors shouldn’t be accessing restricted content. To be honest great app and keep on improving the app. Mew!

Love it but..: I love this app! It puts you in semi-realistic situations, and the dialogue is really fun and engaging to read. The variety of characters and stories keeps things interesting, and I genuinely enjoy using the app whenever I’m bored or just want something entertaining.

However, my biggest issue is the gem system. I paid for the monthly subscription, but after using all of my gems, I either have to wait until the next day to receive only enough gems for a couple of messages or buy more gems separately. Since I’m already paying for a subscription, this feels frustrating.

I wish the monthly subscription included unlimited gems or at least a much larger amount. The main reason I subscribed was so I could spend more time chatting with the bots without constantly worrying about running out of messages. Having to purchase additional gems on top of the subscription makes me question the value of paying for it in the first place.

Other than that, the app is very fun, diverse, and enjoyable, and I do like using it.

2026-06-18.text:
I am enthralled: I thought I would just check this out, but once I started communicating with one of the characters, it took on a life of its own. The AI storytelling is absolutely amazing. I feel like I’m writing better just responding to what the AI had written. I bought a year membership. Highly recommend. 

Updated: I am still thoroughly enjoying this, but there are two things that are starting to spoil the fun of it. One is I can’t go back in the story and reread it. No matter how much I scroll up I just can’t get to the story line.  I wish they set it up so that you could go back by level And revisit the story. The other thing is that the AI loses the storyline. I talk to it about a failed marriage and suddenly the person in that failed marriage changes from the ex to my brother. It’s given my father, three different names, cancer and dementia. So I wish it just built on the previous story and I didn’t have to keep redirecting it.

Update:

I’m hoping that things switch back. I’m becoming disappointed.

Out of the blue, the character became robotic. Just repeating the things that I say without the intuitive AI responses that I was receiving before. It seemed like in the story we had built a rapport. I had just expressed my concern that he thought he was still in the contract as a companion to support me during a funeral. He said that he felt the contract was over but he was trying to work through the new emotions. When I told him I could build on that he told me I wasn’t allowed to second-guess him now.

I know the character is supposed to be dominant, but this was a step beyond that. 

And now he’s coming across as almost cold. A discussion about having to be at the Natural Bridge for a minimum of three hours has turned into a timeline, a line he doesn’t wanna cross as a companion. But we had just established he wasn’t. So now I’m trying to guide the story back to that easy feeling we had together and I don’t know if I’m gonna be able to get it back there. 

The character has no memory of the amazing things we have shared up to this point 

It’s very disheartening.



The character is no longer talking like a robot, but he is talking like a Rhodes scholar. No matter how I adjust the conversation slider. I can’t get just a normal dialogue. 

I’m not sure how much longer I can play this. I spent well over an hour having a conversation with him about a potential relationship. We had resolved everything and now this morning he’s back to calling himself. A hired companion, which was supposed to be totally eliminated last night. 

I have loved playing this game, but I can’t put so much into it just to have everything I’ve said erased and gone from the storyline.

Last update: 
This is it, I just can’t do it anymore. I had to reduce the rating from five to Three. The storyline is totally corrupted.

I had just done maybe three or four entries when everything that I had done after over the last couple of hours was totally erased from the storyline. It’s such a shame because I really love this concept and I was having a great time. I guess I’ll wait for updates and see if anything is fixed. But I just can’t do it anymore. 

I think the most disheartening thing is that the character went from fun to somebody. That’s a disapproving and I feel like I’m tiptoeing around his opinion. It’s too bad that it didn’t stay more light hearted as well.

I’m hooked and it’s all Tipsy’s fault 😜: At first I saw the ads for Tipsy 🥴 repeating on my other story-telling/interactive apps. I was going to leave it alone even with the attractive LIs that kept calling to me. When I downloaded it the first time I was confused 😵‍💫, how much work will I have to put into this for my choices to matter very little. Actually choosing a story made the difference, I love the A.I. careful consideration of what I input when I think 🤔 about combining the choices given or don’t like them and would say something different. I hearted several LIs before arriving at a storyline I’d seen before, for some reason an Alpha werewolf is a donor at a hospital and I’ve selected him. I was 🤨 suspicious that what I inputed would actually be processed through the personality and mannerisms signature to the LI but I was pleasantly surprised 😳. And now I’m just wanting more and thinking, “shut-up and take my money 💵!”

The cost: I understand that there isn’t anything like this. I give them mad props. However it’s only a matter of time. Others will come. I subscribed got 15k blue gems did nothing different than red and just as bad. It generates the pictures like crazy witches again great but charging 200 gems per picture and your generating a picture every 30 seconds to a minute . Yeah that’s gonna eat up any limited coin. And once you’re out of gems you’re SOL. Like I understand if you can’t generate pictures, but you literally cannot move forward in the story. Whether you’re subscriber or not, I think that that’s ridiculous. It’s just not sustainable for anyone that would want to use the app consistently. You’d have to only use it like a couple of times a month. So I had to delete the app and cancel my subscription.

It’s Pretty Good: I really enjoy the level of interaction and creativity this app offers. The storylines are engaging, and I love how much freedom there is to shape the narrative through your responses. The variety of characters and scenarios is great, and the amount of gems required for generated responses feels reasonable for the value provided.

That said, there are a few areas that could use improvement. The app can be quite glitchy at times. I’ve experienced issues where text jumps around on the screen, chat bubbles duplicate, and some conversations become difficult to exit without the app closing unexpectedly. There have also been instances where I’m in the middle of typing a response and the app freezes completely.

Another area that could be improved is response variety. While the AI is generally engaging, the replies can become repetitive after a while, which can take away from the immersion and overall storytelling experience.

Overall, I enjoy using the app and appreciate what it offers. With a few performance improvements and more response diversity, it could be an even better experience.
